card_da_desc: Each framework specifies the precise conditions under which an execution decision is considered valid. Deviations are documented and reviewed as part of the validation cycle.
card_rg_desc: Fixed drawdown thresholds, position sizing constraints, and daily exposure limits are embedded structurally — not applied situationally or adjusted post-hoc.
docs_body: The ALTIVOR documentation suite provides the operational specifications, regulatory structure context, and reference material required to engage with the US100 framework. Documentation access is tiered by plan.
docs_case_desc: A curated collection of anonymized cycle review documents drawn from completed validation cycles. Each case study presents a complete behavioral audit without financial performance data — structure, deviation events, and process outcomes only.
docs_case_note: Exclusive to US100 Challenge. One case study preview available to Framework Pack holders.
docs_fw_desc: The complete operational specification document for the US100 framework. Defines all execution conditions, risk governance parameters, documentation requirements, and validation cycle criteria in full technical detail.
docs_fw_note: Included in Framework Pack and US100 Challenge.
docs_reg_desc: A structured overview of the compliance criteria, behavioral assessment methodology, and validation thresholds applied within the US100 framework. Defines how process consistency is measured and reported.
docs_reg_note: Included in Framework Pack and US100 Challenge.
env_analysis_desc: The designated charting and structural analysis environment. All pre-session structural reference identification and documented level mapping is conducted within TradingView prior to session commencement.
env_body: The US100 framework operates across three distinct functional environments, each serving a specific role in the execution and validation workflow.
env_exec_desc: The designated execution platform for all US100 CFD trades. Order placement, position management, and trade closure occur within MT5 in accordance with the framework's execution constraints.
env_log_desc: The primary data capture and compliance monitoring interface. All trade documentation, cycle tracking, risk governance monitoring, and audit reporting functions are centralized here.
faq_a1: ALTIVOR INSTITUTE is a structural execution institute that develops operational frameworks for systematic market participation. It does not provide signals, advisory services, investment management, or any form of performance guarantee.
faq_a10: Yes. Participants holding Framework Pack access may upgrade to Full Access at any time by completing an additional payment equivalent to the difference in plan pricing.
faq_a4: Access at ALTIVOR INSTITUTE is structured in three layers. First, PREPARE (29 —) — a mandatory one-time procedural qualification gate. Second, a Challenge product (e.g. US100 at 129 —). Third, the Accessories suite — a separate monthly subscription at 79 € / month, discounted to 59 € / month for participants who have successfully passed a challenge.
faq_a5: No. ALTIVOR INSTITUTE does not provide capital, funded accounts, or any form of financial allocation. It provides structural documentation and operational tooling for participants who wish to validate their own execution process.
faq_a6: The Accessories suite is available via a monthly subscription at 79 € / month. Participants who successfully pass a Challenge are permanently eligible for a discounted rate of 59 € / month.
faq_a7: The US100 framework is designed for execution via MetaTrader 5 on a CFD instrument linked to the Nasdaq 100. Structural analysis is conducted via TradingView. ALTIVOR INSTITUTE does not recommend, endorse, or partner with any specific brokerage.
faq_a8: No. ALTIVOR INSTITUTE does not provide market signals, trade recommendations, investment advice, or any form of directional market guidance. All market decisions remain the sole responsibility of the participant.
faq_a9: Payment is processed via Stripe. All transactions are secured via industry-standard encryption. ALTIVOR INSTITUTE does not store card details. Upon successful payment confirmation, an account is created and access is provisioned automatically.
faq_q10: Can I upgrade from Framework Pack to Full Access?
faq_q5: Is this a funded trading programme?
faq_q6: What are the subscription fees for Accessories?
faq_q7: What broker or platform do I need?
faq_q8: Does ALTIVOR provide trading signals or advice?
fw_us30_desc: Currently in formalization phase. Execution parameters, session rules, and risk governance protocols are being developed. Framework is not yet available for participation.
fw_us30_rule1: Parameters pending — execution rules not yet finalized
hcb_btn_prepare: Begin PREPARE <svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 14 14" fill="none"><path d="M3 7h8M8 4l3 3-3 3" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
hero_mini_ad_desc: Prove your strategy in our structured process-validation system. The 45 Trade Cycle is a strategy verification system — designed to determine whether your approach works under disciplined conditions, with fixed risk governance and full documentation standards.
infra_audit_desc: A structured review module that flags protocol deviations, records decision rationale, and maintains a complete chronological audit record across the full validation cycle.
infra_body: The ALTIVOR Operational Panel is the participant's primary interface during an active validation cycle. It is a structured logging and compliance environment — not a performance dashboard. The panel captures process data, not financial results.
infra_log_desc: A structured, session-by-session trade entry interface. Participants document each trade including instrument, direction, entry and exit points, size, and protocol compliance notes.
infra_report_desc: At the end of each calendar month, participants may export a structured PDF behavioral audit report covering all trade activity, compliance metrics, and deviation events within that period.
infra_risk_desc: A live display of current drawdown position relative to defined maximum thresholds. Includes daily exposure utilization and position-level risk allocation monitoring.
infra_score_desc: A per-rule breakdown of compliance across the active validation cycle. Each of the six protocol rules is tracked independently, exposing which specific conditions are generating non-compliant outcomes.
pnl_body: The ALTIVOR PnL Calendar transforms your trade activity into a clear, session-by-session record. Each day is marked with its outcome — logged trades, session result, and protocol compliance status — giving you an unfiltered view of your behavioral patterns across the month.
pnl_feat1_desc: Each calendar day displays trade count and session outcome, color-coded by protocol compliance — not by sentiment.
pnl_feat2_desc: Navigate across weeks and months to review the full arc of a validation cycle. No aggregated noise — only structured session data.
pnl_feat3_desc: Set a monthly reference point and track progress against it without attaching outcome dependency to it. Discipline first, results follow.
pnl_feat4_desc: The calendar is a behavioral mirror. It shows not what you earned, but how consistently you operated within the defined framework across every session.
prep_s1_mandate: Completion of PREPARE is a prerequisite. No participant may access the US100 Challenge without first demonstrating procedural compliance through this qualification stage.
prep_s1_p1: PREPARE is not a performance benchmark. It is a procedural gate — a structural validation mechanism that confirms a participant's ability to execute a declared trading process without deviation, prior to gaining access to the US100 Challenge.
prep_s2_li1: Instrument must be US100 (NASDAQ-100 Index CFD) or approved alias
prep_s2_li2: Stop Loss must be set at entry (mandatory)
prep_s2_li3: Trade must be opened during NY session (09:30–16:00 ET)
prep_s2_li4: Maximum 10 lot position size per trade
prep_s2_li5: MT5 History screenshot required for each submission
prep_s2_li6: Data must match MT5 Account History exactly
prep_s2_p1: To pass PREPARE, you must submit <strong>10 fully compliant trades
prep_s3_p2: This mechanism exists to prevent reactive execution patterns following consecutive procedural failures. Access is automatically restored after the cooldown expires.
prep_s4_p1: The following are explicitly excluded from the PREPARE qualification framework:
price_acc_desc: Monthly access to the full Accessories suite — Trading Log, PnL Calendar, Economic Calendar, Trading Symbols, Execution Checklist, and Calculators.
price_body: Access to ALTIVOR INSTITUTE is structured in two layers: a mandatory procedural qualification (PREPARE), followed by a Challenge product of your choice. Operational tools — the Accessories suite — are available separately.
price_discount_notice: Pass your Challenge and unlock <strong>Accessories at 59 € / month
price_fwpack_desc: Documentation access with full operational framework specification. Framework documents and case study library without the interactive Operational Panel.
price_note: Access to Accessories is subscription-based and renews monthly. It is not included in any challenge product. Passing any ALTIVOR challenge unlocks the discounted rate of 59 € / month for 12 months — applicable across all Accessories options. Subscribing directly at 79 € / month requires no PREPARE qualification, no challenge participation, and no compliance rules — pure standalone access.
price_prepare_desc: Mandatory procedural qualification gate. Required before any challenge. Validates structural readiness and execution discipline baseline.
price_step1_desc: Mandatory procedural gate. Universal — applies to all challenges. One-time.
price_step2_desc: US100 active. US30 in development. Unlocked only after PREPARE.
price_step3_desc: Available separately. Discounted to 59 € / mo on successful challenge pass.
price_us100_desc: Full operational access to the US100 framework — documentation, execution panel, behavioral audit system, and validation cycle infrastructure.
price_us100_li7: Accessories Suite — 59 € / mo on pass for 12 months
sup_sub: Our dedicated support team is available <strong>Monday to Saturday
sup_vis_msg1: Welcome to ALTIVOR. How can I assist you with the US100 framework today?
sup_vis_msg4: Which plan gives me access to the full documentation?
sup_vis_msg5: Both the Framework Pack and the US100 Challenge include the full documentation suite. The Challenge also unlocks the Operational Panel.
wiki_bb_desc: An Order Block that was violated — price traded through it, invalidating it as a support/resistance. The breaker now acts in the opposite direction. A bullish OB that fails becomes a bearish breaker, and vice versa.
wiki_bpr_desc: An area where two opposing FVGs overlap. The overlap zone represents near-perfect price equilibrium. Strong reaction area due to double institutional interest.
wiki_ce_desc: The exact midpoint (50%) of a Fair Value Gap. Price frequently reacts precisely at this level. Used for precision stop placement and entry refinement within the FVG zone.
wiki_fib_50_desc: Fair value midpoint of the range. Price frequently rotates here.
wiki_fib_62_desc: Where many retail traders place entries. Institutions are aware of this clustering.
wiki_fib_705_desc: High-probability reaction zone. Often aligns with OB or FVG entries.
wiki_fib_79_desc: Maximum pain zone for retail. Institutions frequently enter here — small SL, large RR. Retail stops placed at 61.8% are swept before the real move begins.
wiki_fib_warn_body: Buying in Premium zones and selling in Discount zones — entering after the move is already delivered, not before it begins.
wiki_fvg_desc: A three-candle imbalance: the wick of candle 1 and the wick of candle 3 do not overlap, leaving a price gap on candle 2. This zone represents undelivered institutional orders. 70—80% of impulsive moves return to fill the FVG before continuation.
wiki_kz_warn_body: Setups that form outside these windows carry significantly lower probability. Entering mid-session without a Kill Zone catalyst is a common retail execution error.
wiki_liq_bsl_desc: Stop-losses of short sellers and breakout buy orders clustered above swing highs, equal highs, and session highs. Target for bearish institutions seeking fill.
wiki_liq_dol_desc: The nearest logical price target — the next liquidity pool price is drawn toward. Identifies where price is heading before a potential reversal. Always assess DOL before entry.
wiki_liq_eql_desc: Two or more consecutive highs or lows at the same price level. Visually obvious to retail — therefore high-probability liquidity targets. Institutions sweep these levels routinely.
wiki_liq_lqs_desc: Price momentarily breaks a liquidity level, collects the orders, then rapidly reverses. The wick beyond the level is the institutional fingerprint. Confirmation of sweep requires a close back inside the range.
wiki_liq_note_body: Price always moves toward liquidity. If you cannot identify where the liquidity is, you cannot identify where price is going.
wiki_liq_ssl_desc: Stop-losses of long holders and breakout sell orders clustered below swing lows, equal lows, and session lows. Target for bullish institutions seeking fill.
wiki_man_ind_desc: A minor liquidity pool deliberately left visible to draw retail into entering before the actual institutional move. Price sweeps the inducement, collects orders, then delivers in the true direction.
wiki_man_js_desc: A false directional move engineered at the start of a session to trigger retail entries and stop-losses in the wrong direction. Typically occurs in the first 5—15 minutes of London Open or NY Open. The trap is set before the real move begins.
wiki_man_lv_desc: A large, fast candle with no pullback — price moved through an area without creating balanced two-sided price delivery. Markets statistically return to fill these voids as institutions re-enter at better prices.
wiki_mit_desc: Price returns to an institutional zone (OB, FVG) after displacement to allow remaining institutional orders to be filled. This return is the structured entry opportunity — not the initial displacement candle.
wiki_ob_desc: The last opposing candle before a displacement move that breaks structure. A valid OB must: (1) cause a BOS, (2) be preceded by displacement, (3) leave an FVG, and (4) be located in a premium or discount zone. Not simply "the last bearish candle."
wiki_psy_early_desc: Entering before displacement completes and FVG forms. Anticipating the setup rather than waiting for confirmation. The setup does not exist until the structural sequence is complete.
wiki_psy_fomo_desc: Retail chases breakouts above equal highs and below equal lows — entering exactly where institutions are exiting. The breakout is the manipulation candle, not the entry signal.
wiki_psy_note_body: Behavioral consistency — waiting for the full sequential setup, respecting Kill Zones, never deviating from defined risk — is not a soft skill. It is the operational edge. Process adherence is the only variable within the operator's control.
wiki_psy_revenge_desc: After a loss, emotional re-entry to recover capital. Produces a second loss from a sub-threshold setup. Institutional rule: zero re-entries after stopped trades. The next setup is independent of the prior outcome.
wiki_psy_stops_desc: Retail consistently places stops below obvious swing lows (for longs) and above obvious swing highs (for shorts). These become the exact liquidity targets for institutional sweeps.
wiki_reac_desc: A consolidation within a bullish trend before continuation. Institutions re-add to positions during range. Appears as a ranging market — retail interprets as reversal, institutions use it for position building.
wiki_redist_desc: A consolidation within a bearish trend before continuation lower. Mirror of reaccumulation. Institutions distribute (sell) remaining longs into retail buy orders generated by the temporary range.
wiki_risk_bias_desc: No counter-trend entries unless a confirmed CHoCH on HTF has occurred. Fading a trend without structural confirmation is a behavioral failure, not a strategy.
wiki_risk_kz_desc: No entries outside defined Kill Zones. Trading mid-session is reactive, not systematic. The absence of a Kill Zone catalyst removes the statistical edge from the setup.
wiki_risk_pct_desc: 1—2% of account equity maximum. Fixed fractional sizing. Position size is calculated backward from stop-loss distance — not forward from desired lot size.
wiki_risk_rr_desc: 1:3 minimum. A 1:3 RR structure allows profitability at a 34% win rate. Below this threshold, the mathematical edge of the model degrades. Do not take sub-1:3 setups.
wiki_s01_note_body: Price moves toward liquidity — not because "the market wants to" — but because institutions require those orders to execute. Every sweep, every false breakout, every Judas swing is a function of this mechanic.
wiki_s01_p1: Retail participants do not move price. Price is moved by entities with sufficient capital to create genuine order flow: banks, hedge funds, and institutional asset managers. Their structural problem is size — they require massive liquidity to open and close positions without self-disruption.
wiki_s01_p2: Liquidity is not abstract. It is specifically located where retail stop-losses are clustered — below equal lows, above equal highs, at round numbers and session extremes. Institutions need these orders to fill their own.
wiki_s01_p3: This is why price visits equal highs before reversing, why London sweeps the Asian range, and why 15:30 Polish time produces aggressive displacement. The mechanism is always the same: collect liquidity, then deliver price.
wiki_s02_note: This cycle repeats on every timeframe, every day.
wiki_s02_p1: Every directional move in the market — regardless of timeframe — follows a four-phase institutional delivery cycle. Understanding this cycle defines where you are in the market narrative at any given moment.
wiki_s04_p1: The three primary market sessions are not equal in terms of institutional participation. Each serves a distinct function in the daily liquidity cycle.
wiki_s05_p1: Kill Zones are time windows with the highest statistical probability of institutional movement. Capital enters, liquidity is collected, and displacement is generated within these windows.
wiki_s06_ext_p: Major swings on H1—D1. Defines the macro bias — the institutional direction. Trade only in alignment with external structure unless a confirmed CHoCH has occurred.
wiki_s06_int_p: Micro BOS/CHoCH formations on M1—M15. Used for timing precise entries within the macro narrative. Advanced operators use HTF for direction and LTF for execution timing.
wiki_s06_p1: Market structure is the framework that defines directional bias. Institutional operators operate on at least two structural levels simultaneously: external (macro) and internal (micro).
wiki_s07_p1: Liquidity is the singular driver of institutional price delivery. All price movement is a function of liquidity collection followed by displacement. Understanding where liquidity pools exist is the foundation of institutional analysis.
wiki_s09_disp_p: A valid displacement has: large candle bodies with minimal wicks, sequential momentum candles (not a single spike), a structural break (BOS or CHoCH), and leaves at least one FVG. It is the institutional footprint.
wiki_s09_p1: Displacement is the physical evidence of institutional capital entering the market. It manifests as a series of large-bodied candles with minimal wicks, breaking structure and creating Fair Value Gaps in its wake.
wiki_s11_p1: The Dealing Range is defined between a significant swing high and swing low. This range is divided into Premium (upper half) and Discount (lower half) relative to the 50% Equilibrium. Institutional logic: buy in Discount, sell in Premium.
wiki_s12_example_body: Asia builds range — London sweeps SSL (bear trap) — 15:30 NY Open: price sweeps BSL (equal highs) — Judas Swing upward in first 5 min — Displacement downward with FVG created — Price retraces to FVG (70—79% Fib) — Short entry — Target: low of day (SSL).
wiki_s12_p1: The institutional entry model is a sequential checklist. Each condition must be confirmed in order. Skipping steps degrades the probability profile of the trade.
wiki_s13_p1: Institutional risk management is not flexible. Parameters are defined before session start and do not change based on outcome. Deviation from defined parameters is a process failure — regardless of whether the deviation generated profit.
wiki_s14_p1: Retail participants behave predictably — and institutions exploit this predictability systematically. Understanding how retail thinking creates liquidity is as important as understanding the technical setup itself.
wiki_sess_asia_desc: Low volatility. Builds the daily range. Institutions prepare the trap — equal highs and lows form, creating visible liquidity targets for London to sweep.
wiki_sess_london_desc: High bank capital. Frequently sweeps Asian session highs or lows. Sets the directional bias for the day. London Open (08:00—09:00) is a primary Kill Zone.
wiki_sess_note_body: NYSE opens at 15:30 Polish time. Funds rebalance portfolios, execute overnight orders, and hedge options. This produces real displacement candles on US100. Scalpers wait 5—15 minutes before the first candle — the initial 5 minutes is the highest-probability Judas zone.
wiki_sess_ny_desc: Highest volume session. NYSE opening at 15:30 CET triggers real equity capital inflows. NASDAQ-100 (US100) reacts to actual institutional order flow — generating displacement.
wiki_str_bos_desc: Candle close beyond a significant swing high/low in the direction of trend. Confirms continuation. Not a reversal signal — structure is intact.
wiki_str_choch_desc: The first structural break against the prevailing trend. Not confirmation of reversal — it is the first signal that the narrative may be changing. Requires additional confirmation.
wiki_str_hh_desc: Higher High (HH) + Higher Low (HL). Each swing high exceeds the prior, each swing low holds above prior. Confirms bullish trend continuation.
wiki_str_ll_desc: Lower High (LH) + Lower Low (LL). Each swing high fails below prior, each low breaks prior low. Confirms bearish trend continuation.
wiki_style_day_desc: Positions opened and closed within the same session. Based on session liquidity dynamics, HTF bias, and intraday structure. No overnight exposure.
wiki_style_scalp_desc: Very short positions (seconds to minutes), exploiting microstructure and rapid liquidity sweeps. Requires precise timing, low latency execution, and strict session discipline. Best applied within Kill Zones.
wiki_style_swing_desc: Multi-day to multi-week positions. Based on higher timeframe structure, weekly liquidity targets, and macro bias. Requires tolerance for drawdown and overnight risk.
wiki_sub: Institutional market structure concepts, liquidity mechanics, and execution models. A reference framework for structured, process-consistent trading.
